title: Tyga Introduces New Artist Persona $TARFACE With ‘GAVE U RACKS’
summary: "I wanted this character and music to live on its own," says Tyga.
body: Tyga is stepping into an entirely new creative universe with $TARFACE — a character-driven project rooted in 1980s synth-wave, pop, R&B, funk and cinematic storytelling that the rapper says is designed to exist completely separately from his established catalogue. The debut single “ GAVE U RACKS ,” alongside its official music video, is out now via Empire. “I wanted this character and music to live on its own,” Tyga says. “This needed to exist separately, to give me complete freedom creatively.” Rather than a standard Tyga release, $TARFACE is a fully realised alternate persona built around a fictional early-1980s world loosely inspired by the mythology and visual language of Scarface — while telling an entirely original story. The aesthetic is early-’80s Miami: silk tailoring, white suits, vintage luxury, nightlife, romance and excess. The 10-track project features “Lavish” with Fenix Flexin and is intentionally vocal-led — more sung than rapped — with warm analogue synths, pitched vocals, soulful melodies and vintage production techniques crafted to sound as though the music could have been released in 1983 rather than bo…

title: From ‘Royal Wedding’ Mugs to ‘Paper Rings,’ Taylor & Travis Wedding Merch Has Already Hit the Internet
summary: These are all unofficial items of course, but well, they're actually romantic.
body: All products and services featured are independently chosen by editors. However, Billboard may receive a commission on orders placed through its retail links, and the retailer may receive certain auditable data for accounting purposes. It’s official: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are now husband and wife. The duo tied the knot Saturday (June 3) at Madison Square Garden in New York City, in a star-studded ceremony that was officiated by Adam Sandler . The guest list, meantime, reportedly included Selena Gomez, Ed Sheeran, Sabrina Carpenter, the HAIM sisters and Kelce’s Kansas City Chiefs teammates. And yes, the bride wore white, in a custom wedding dress by Jonathan Anderson for Christian Dior Haute Couture . As details slowly trickle out about the ceremony — with photos no doubt soon to follow — savvy Swifties have already taken to the internet to create their own wedding merch and wedding gifts inspired by pop culture’s reigning royal couple. These are all unofficial items of course, but well, they’re actually romantic , and make great wedding gifts for Taylor Swift fans, or fun collectibles for pop culture fans, whether you’re single, ta…

title: Here’s Everything We Know About Taylor Swift & Travis Kelce’s Wedding So Far
summary: From the size of the guest list to when they tied the knot, here's what has been revealed from Tayvis' big day.
body: When Taylor Swif t and Travis Kelce announced their engagement with a simple Instagram post in August 2025, it broke the internet. And in the time since, the superstar couple continued to turn heads. Swift released her 15th Billboard 200 No. 1 album, The Life of a Showgirl , that October — moving a record-breaking 4 million equivalent album units in its first week — while Kelce signed on for his 14th season with the Kansas City Chiefs the following April. Related A Timeline of Taylor Swift & Travis Kelce’s Relationship Members of the Tayvis camp also kept what they may or may not have known about the couple’s plans mostly quiet. Kelce’s mother, Donna Kelce , straight up refused to speak about her potential involvement with the wedding planning when approached by a photographer at LAX. “I’m just happy. I’m so happy for them,” Donna said at the time. Swift’s sister-in-law, Kylie Kelce (who’s married to Trav’s big brother, Jason) even made a plea on her Not Gonna Lie podcast in April for people to stop asking her for wedding dirt. “Quit asking me and my mother-in-law about upcoming nuptials,”…

title: Madonna and Daughter Lola Leon Discuss ‘Confessions II’ Collab ‘The Test’: ‘A Very Magical Moment’
summary: The track marks the mother-daughter duo's first musical team-up.
body: Madonna and her daughter, Lola Leon , are opening up about the emotional experience of collaborating on Confessions II song “The Test.” During an interview Thursday (July 2) with Bob the Drag Queen on “iHeartRadio and TikTok LIVE Premiere With Madonna,” the mother-daughter duo discussed the making of the song, which was written by Madonna and Leon, produced by Stuart Price, with additional production from Arca. Related Madonna’s ‘Confessions II’: All 16 Tracks Ranked Madonna Is Back With ‘Confessions II’: Stream It Now Madonna Gifting Fans an Early Listen of ‘Confessions II’ Album Through Thursday Livestream: All the Details Bob the Drag Queen kicked off the conversation by asking whose idea it was to create “The Test,” which marks the Queen of Pop’s first official musical team-up with her 29-year-old eldest daughter. “It was my idea,” Leon responded, explaining that “there’s a lot of things that are unspoken” in a mother-daughter relationship. View this post on Instagram “There’s a lot of love that’s unspoken, but also a lot of tension and emotion…

title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: A predawn Moon-and-planets meetup, a returning comet, a great chance to see the Milky Way, and Saturn’s rings at a new angle. Skywatching Highlights Transcript An early morning hangout with the Moon and planets, a comet swings by, prime time for the Milky Way, and Saturn’s rings shine at a new angl…
body: Skywatching Skywatching Home What’s Up Meteor Showers Eclipses Daily Moon Guide More Tips & Guides Skywatching FAQ Night Sky Network A predawn Moon-and-planets meetup, a returning comet, a great chance to see the Milky Way, and Saturn’s rings at a new angle. Skywatching Highlights July 7: Last Quarter Moon July 11 + 12: Dawn alignment of the Moon, Mars, Saturn, and Uranus July 14: New Moon; best dark-sky window for Comet 10P/Tempel 2 and the Milky Way Later in July: Saturn’s unusually thin rings are a rewarding telescope target July 21: First Quarter Moon July 29: Full Moon Transcript An early morning hangout with the Moon and planets, a comet swings by, prime time for the Milky Way, and Saturn’s rings shine at a new angle. That’s What’s Up for July. Before sunrise on July 11 and 12, look toward the eastern sky for a lineup of the Moon and planets. On these mornings, the waning crescent Moon helps point the way to Mars, with Saturn shining nearby in the morning sky. Uranus is in the same general part of the sky, too, but it is much fainter, so you will need binoculars or a telescope to see it. Mars will look like a small reddish point of light, Saturn is brighter and easier t…
